title: SORT_RO
description: Returns the sorted elements of a list, a set, or a sorted set.
syntax:
SORT_RO key [BY pattern] [LIMIT offset count] [GET pattern [GET
  pattern ...]] [ASC | DESC] [ALPHA]
complexity: O(N+M*log(M)) where N is the number of elements in the list or set to sort, and M the number of returned elements. When the elements are not sorted, complexity is O(N).

redis_py.signature: sort_ro(key: str, start: Optional[int] = None, num: Optional[int] = None, by: Optional[str] = None, get: Optional[List[str]] = None, desc: bool = False, alpha: bool = False)

keySpecs.notes: For the optional BY/GET keyword. It is marked 'unknown' because the key names derive from the content of the key we sort

content:
Read-only variant of the [`SORT`]() command. It is exactly like the original [`SORT`]() but refuses the `STORE` option and can safely be used in read-only replicas. Since the original [`SORT`]() has a `STORE` option it is technically flagged as a writing command in the Redis command table. For this reason read-only replicas in a Redis Cluster will redirect it to the master instance even if the connection is in read-only mode (see the [`READONLY`]() command of Redis Cluster). The `SORT_RO` variant was introduced in order to allow [`SORT`]() behavior in read-only replicas without breaking compatibility on command flags. See original [`SORT`]() for more details. ## Examples ``` SORT_RO mylist BY weight_*->fieldname GET object_*->fieldname ``` ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="sort-ro-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of sorted elements. -tab-sep- [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of sorted elements. {{< /multitabs >}}
example: SORT_RO mylist BY weight_*->fieldname GET object_*->fieldname
intro:
Read-only variant of the [`SORT`]() command. It is exactly like the original [`SORT`]() but refuses the `STORE` option and can safely be used in read-only replicas.

Since the original [`SORT`]() has a `STORE` option it is technically flagged as a writing command in the Redis command table. For this reason read-only replicas in a Redis Cluster will redirect it to the master instance even if the connection is in read-only mode (see the [`READONLY`]() command of Redis Cluster).

The `SORT_RO` variant was introduced in order to allow [`SORT`]() behavior in read-only replicas without breaking compatibility on command flags.

See original [`SORT`]() for more details.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Read-only variant of the [`SORT`]() command. It is exactly like the original [`SORT`]() but refuses the `STORE` option and can safely be used in read-only replicas.

Since the original [`SORT`]() has a `STORE` option it is technically flagged as a writing command in the Redis command table. For this reason read-only replicas in a Redis Cluster will redirect it to the master instance even if the connection is in read-only mode (see the [`READONLY`]() command of Redis Cluster).

The `SORT_RO` variant was introduced in order to allow [`SORT`]() behavior in read-only replicas without breaking compatibility on command flags.

See original [`SORT`]() for more details.

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
```
SORT_RO mylist BY weight_*->fieldname GET object_*->fieldname
```
